description: §12.4 — a client-only handler (`refresh`, touches `document`) CALLING a server-classified fn (`auditName`, ?{} SQL) fires no E-ROUTE-002. client -> server is the allowed direction (the compiler emits a fetch stub, §12.3); the server fn itself calls no client-only function. The negative control for the E-ROUTE-002 pos case — asserts the reject does NOT over-fire on the canonical safe pattern (a false positive would break valid code).
rationale: §12.4 constrains only the server -> client-only direction. A client-pinned function may freely call a server-escalated function (§12.3 fetch stub); the boundary is not crossed the wrong way, so E-ROUTE-002 stays silent.
